facts.text: Red Creek Historical Association, founded in 1972, preserves the town’s past and operates out of the 1880 Old Schoolhouse.
The association restored the Old Schoolhouse and the Red Creek Village Hall, keeping key historic buildings alive for future generations.
A research library and archives in the Old Schoolhouse hold photographs, documents, and artifacts that chronicle Red Creek’s history.
In the late 1700s, Native Mohicans enjoyed the same landscape that later became Red Creek, as noted in Hendrick Aupaumut’s 1791 account.
The Stockbridge‑Munsee Band of Mohicans considers Dutchess County, including Red Creek, an area of archaeological interest.
Land patents granted after the War of Independence were bought by Gorham and Phelps, early settlers who helped shape Red Creek’s development.
